Right midlung opacity with air bronchograms compatible with airspace disease or atelectasis; right pleural effusion and/or thickening noted along minor fissure, possibly representing loculated fluid/pleura versus scarring. Right hemidiaphragm elevation consistent with volume loss from prior surgery (e.g., wedge resection). Left lung clear without focal consolidation. Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits. No acute osseous abnormality identified.

Small to moderate-sized right-sided pneumothorax vs small-to-moderate sized hydropneumothorax overlying major fissures due to adjacent parenchymal opacities suggesting possible subsegmental collapse of lingula/lung base. Recommend follow-up imaging if clinically indicated given differential diagnosis between postoperative changes/scars versus active pathology such as pneumonia/hemothorax/pulmonary contusions/injury.

**Findings:**  
No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax identified. Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits and pulmonary vascularity unremarkable. Atherosclerotic calcifications of thoracic aorta noted. Degenerative changes throughout spine bilaterally with mild degenerative disc disease at L5/S1 level. No acute osseous abnormality visualized. Right-sided PICC catheter tip projects over mid superior vena cava region without evidence of malposition.  

**Impression:**  
Right upper extremity central venous catheter remains appropriately positioned; no complications evident. Mild lumbar spondylosis including anterior osteophytes and posterior marginal spurring along lower endplate facets between vertebral bodies L4-L6. Left shoulder demonstrates chronic posttraumatic deformities consistent with healed fracture healing process. Thoracolumbar junction shows moderate to severe compression fractures involving multiple levels (T9-T12) which may be sequelae from prior trauma but appear stable given lack of interval change since initial imaging performed approximately one year ago following patient’s admission two years after original injury event.

**Findings:**  
Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits. No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality identified except as described below.  

Right lower lobe airspace disease with right pleural effusion and possible small left-sided pleural fluid collection; no pneumothorax seen. Right apical scarring or granuloma noted, possibly representing old tuberculosis infection versus neoplasm. Multiple surgical clips overlying upper abdomen likely related to prior abdominal surgery. Degenerative changes of thoracic spine compatible with age-related osteoarthritis.  


**Impression:**  
Small-to-moderate bilateral pleural collections (right greater than left) without definite evidence of active pulmonary process at present time. Differential diagnosis includes postoperative change from recent lung resection vs. residual inflammatory infiltrates/consolidation secondary to pneumonia. Follow-up imaging recommended if clinically indicated.

**Findings:**  
Lung volumes low with bibasilar atelectasis and scarring, left greater than right; no focal airspace disease or pneumothorax identified. Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits postoperative changes from prior sternotomy including intact midline sternal wires without acute osseous abnormality noted. Clinical correlation recommended if symptoms persist despite imaging interpretation limitations due to technical factors such as patient positioning during exam acquisition.

l3_raw:
Findings: Right upper lobe mass lesion with surrounding airspace disease and volume loss consistent with pneumonia or tumor infiltrate, left lung clear without focal consolidation. No pleural effusion seen. Heart size within normal limits. Aortic calcification noted along thoracic aorta. Degenerative changes of spine visible throughout lumbar region. Large gastric air bubble present below diaphragm level II. Left shoulder joint degeneration visualized bilaterally.

Impression: Mass-like opacity overlying right apex likely representing an area of infection (pneumonia) versus neoplasm; follow-up imaging recommended to exclude malignancy given its location near major vessels/bronchi. Clear lungs otherwise. Normal heart size. Thoracic atherosclerotic vascular disease. Spinal osteophytes at L4-L5/L5-S1 levels. Bilateral degenerative findings involving both shoulders. Gastric distention secondary to large bowel obstruction from ileus is unlikely based upon current presentation but may be related to recent abdominal surgery performed two weeks ago prior to study date.
